conventions: Peer-multiple relative valuation, probability-weighted across scenarios. Valuation only — no live market price is used.

drivers.label: FY2027 EPS
drivers.comment: This is the swing factor for the whole valuation, and the Street does not agree with itself. Post-Q3'26, the newer S&P Global / StockAnalysis cut runs FY2027 EPS ~$150 while the older LSEG/MarketScreener cut sits near ~$98, with a June S&P research note at ~$112. Base $115 sits deliberately in the conservative middle; Bull $150 takes the high consensus at face value; Bear $75 assumes the HBM supercycle rolls over before FY2027 prints. The multiples are peer-anchored and stable — it is this denominator, not the multiple, that decides the answer.

drivers.label: FY2027 EBITDA
drivers.verdict: Tracks EPS; margin at cycle peak
drivers.comment: ~70% (Bear) to ~83% (Bull) EBITDA margin on the revenue line — extraordinary, and only defensible at a memory up-cycle peak with HBM sold out and DRAM/NAND in structural shortage. These are peak-cycle, not mid-cycle, figures; that is the point of a relative-valuation snapshot for a cyclical, but it also means the EV/EBITDA read inherits full cyclical risk.

drivers.label: FY2027 revenue
drivers.comment: $150B (Bear) to $235B (Bull) spans the published FY2027 revenue consensus range (~$169B older cut to ~$234B newer cut). Base $195B is mid-range. HBM is ~2026 sold out with multi-year agreements extending into 2027–28, which underpins the near term; the risk is entirely on 2028+ supply coming back.

drivers.label: FY2027 book value / share
drivers.comment: Book value per share climbs quickly as huge retained earnings pile up ($150 → $180 across scenarios). That mechanically drags the P/B-implied price down and is exactly why P/B is the weakest anchor here — see the P/B multiple note.

drivers.label: P/E multiple
drivers.verdict: Premium to peers, justified in part
drivers.comment: 8× (Bear) / 10× (Base) / 15× (Bull) sits above the SK Hynix ~5.4× / Samsung ~4.9× legacy-memory anchor. HSBC documents a ~35% average MU premium to SK Hynix over 13 years for real reasons — US listing / investor access, friendlier capital return, higher beta on a smaller earnings base. Base 10× embeds a modest re-rating toward an 'AI-infrastructure' frame; Bull 15× matches the last cycle top.

drivers.label: EV/EBITDA multiple
drivers.comment: 6× (Bear) / 8× (Base) / 12× (Bull) brackets the peer set (SK Hynix ~7.7×, Samsung ~8.6×) and MU's own 13-year median of ~6.8×. Bear returns to the historical mid; Bull assumes a full 'new-paradigm' re-rate. This and P/E are the two core anchors — least distorted by the balance sheet.

drivers.label: P/B multiple
drivers.comment: 2.5× / 4× / 6× against SK Hynix ~3.5× and Samsung ~2.0× (a diversified conglomerate, so not clean). P/B is memory's classic trough tool, but here MU's ROE is ~60% vs Samsung ~19%, so a flat peer P/B systematically under-values it — high ROE earns a higher P/B. Treat the P/B row as a floor / downside reference, not a fair value; the headline equal-weights it in for conservatism, and the 'core' cross-check drops it.

drivers.label: EV/Sales multiple
drivers.comment: 3.5× / 5× / 7× vs peers ~4.0–6.1×. EV/Sales ignores the enormous margin differences between up- and down-cycle, so it is a coarse sanity check rather than a primary anchor — included in the blend but not in the core.

drivers.label: Net cash (cash − debt)
drivers.comment: ~$25B net cash (cash + investments ~$30B less debt ~$5.7B post-upgrade to BBB+). Used flat across scenarios in the EV→equity bridge. It is deliberately conservative: FY2027 free cash flow (consensus ~$70B) would build net cash far above this, but much of it is earmarked for buybacks, which cut both net cash and the share count — so leaving it flat is the honest simplification.

drivers.label: Diluted shares
drivers.comment: ~1.13B diluted shares, held flat. Buybacks would trim this over time (mildly accretive), but pinning it avoids double-counting against the flat net-cash assumption above.

drivers.label: Scenario probability
drivers.comment: Bear 25 / Base 45 / Bull 30. The weighting deliberately carries real cyclical downside risk — memory has repeatedly given back peak earnings once supply catches up (revenue fell ~40% into 2023), and multiple analysts still question MU's moat and note recent DRAM/NAND share slippage. The 30% Bull acknowledges genuine HBM momentum and sold-out capacity without treating a multi-year supercycle as the base rate. The blended weighted fair value (~$1,068) lands just above the Base case; the honest read is that this is a fairly-valued-to-modestly-cheap name with a very wide, cycle-dependent distribution, not a high-conviction point estimate.
